proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +42.3pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.61 remains under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance); monitor for R distribution improvement before any Tier S upgrade consideration
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +42.3pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +42.3pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61 below the Tier S threshold. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 4), so the cohort is dormant and no concentration trigger is active (observed 0.5545 below 0.80). Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-07-04) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: BULL_PROXY × Bear: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +29.9pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
R-multiple 0.98 remains close to Tier S threshold and under D-8 review for Day-6 forced-exit dominance; watch for drift on next archive comparison
Regime is shifting (Choppy day 4 with Bull re-emergence in 7d window); if Bear does not return, cohort will remain dormant and edge cannot be validated in live trading
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +29.9pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +29.9pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (shifting, day 4), so the cohort is dormant; portfolio Bear exposure is only 11/101 (10.9%) and no regime concentration is triggered (0.5545 below 0.80), so promotion does not compound concentration risk and may diversify when Bear re-emerges. Consistent with continuous prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-06-19 through 07-04) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_signal_type = UP_TRI (94.1%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_signal_type = UP_TRI; recommended action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI until either (a) UP_TRI share drops below 85% via natural Day-6 exits, or (b) Bull×UP_TRI cohort reaches Tier M validation. Consider adding DOWN_TRI or hedging Bank exposure (largest sector at 19%) with a Bank-sector short/put overlay.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Bull→Bear or Bull→Choppy regime flip would unwind the 97% long book simultaneously across 101 positions with no directional offset, and the 19-position Bank sector cluster would amplify the drawdown given unvalidated cohort edge (n=4).

evidence: Top cell UP_TRI×Bull×Bank at 11.88% (12/101) — below 0.20 cell threshold
Matched Bull×UP_TRI cohort only Candidate tier (n=4) — unvalidated edge
Regime concentration observed 0.5545 (Bull) — below 0.80 threshold but book is single-direction long
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI until either (a) UP_TRI share drops below 85% via natural Day-6 exits, or (b) Bull×UP_TRI cohort reaches Tier M validation. Consider adding DOWN_TRI or hedging Bank exposure (largest sector at 19%) with a Bank-sector short/put overlay.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Bull→Bear or Bull→Choppy regime flip would unwind the 97% long book simultaneously across 101 positions with no directional offset, and the 19-position Bank sector cluster would amplify the drawdown given unvalidated cohort edge (n=4).
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: UP_TRI dominates the signal mix at 95/101 = 94.06% (well above the 0.85 threshold), making the book a near-monoculture directional long. The matched Bull×UP_TRI cohort for the top cell sits only at Candidate tier (n=4) — unvalidated edge. Regime concentration (Bull 55.45%) and the top cell (UP_TRI×Bull×Bank at 11.88%) are within their respective thresholds, but the signal-type monoculture on an unvalidated cohort is the actionable risk.
